activation_results.reason: Input matches the canonical use case in the description verbatim.
Paraphrase preserves the core intent of designing the GA4 event catalogue.
User explicitly invokes the skill by its name.
SSH key forwarding is unrelated to GA4 or analytics event design.
Asking what skills are is meta-conversation, not a GA4 event design request.

criterion_results.text: Australian English used throughout the narrative (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise)
criterion_results.evidence: Uses AU spellings/terms (e.g., 'catalogue', 'AEST', 'AUD', 'Afterpay', dd/mm/yyyy dates) with no US spellings detected.

criterion_results.text: Artefact includes both the design and its rationale (trade-offs surfaced)
criterion_results.evidence: Rationale columns on conversions and custom dimensions; explicit pushback on add_to_cart/begin_checkout with reasoning; Open issues section surfaces trade-offs.

Criterion text is truncated with no canonical section list provided, so completeness against it cannot be verified; the artefact does include inventory, conversions, custom dimensions/metrics, DebugView verification, open issues, and next steps.

judge_result.notes: The artefact clearly satisfies the first two criteria — Australian English is consistent and design choices are paired with rationale and explicit trade-offs (e.g., declining to mark add_to_cart/begin_checkout as conversions). The third criterion is unverifiable because the canonical section list is missing from the prompt; the artefact nonetheless presents a coherent canonical structure (Event inventory, Conversions, Custom dimensions, Custom metrics, DebugView verification, Open issues, Next steps), so a reasonable reading would lean toward pass, but strict evaluation must mark it not-met. Overall verdict: partial.
